analyst.question: Will BTC trade above $120,000 on any day in Q4 2026?

reasoning.text: BTC is currently trading in the $95,000–$105,000 range in May 2026, approximately 15–25% below the $120,000 threshold. Q4 2026 is still ~5 months away, giving considerable time for price appreciation.

Historical BTC cycle patterns show strong Q4 seasonality. In prior bull cycles (2020, 2021), BTC posted its largest gains in Q4. The current halving cycle (April 2024 halving) historically peaks 12–18 months post-halving, placing peak cycle timing squarely in Q4 2026.

From a ~$100K baseline, reaching $120K requires only a ~20% move. BTC has historically achieved 20%+ moves within a single quarter during bull markets. The probability of at least touching $120K on one day across a 92-day window is substantially higher than the probability of sustaining it.
Liquidity is $283,000 and 24h volume is $712,400 — well above the $25K liquidity threshold and highly liquid. Slippage risk is minimal at typical position sizes.

Key risks: macro deterioration (Fed tightening, risk-off), regulatory shock, or a bear market extension could suppress BTC below $120K through year-end. If BTC fails to sustain the current bull cycle, this resolves NO. Also, 'daily high on Coinbase' is precise — intraday wicks count, which slightly increases YES probability vs. close-based resolution.

Market prices YES at 34%. My fair value is ~52%, implying ~18 percentage points of edge (1,800 bps). With $283K liquidity and a clear cyclical/structural thesis, this is a compelling BUY_YES. The market appears to be underweighting halving-cycle tailwinds and the relatively modest 20% move required from current levels.
